Good evening, and welcome to A Cozinha do Porto. Here is the menu. Would you like something to drink?
Good evening. Yes, please. We would like a bottle of sparkling water.
Of course. Are you ready to order, or would you like a few more minutes?
Almost. I do not know these dishes. What do you recommend for the main course?
The grilled fish. It is our special tonight, it is fresher than the meat, and it is cheaper.
Perfect. We would like two tomato soups for the appetizer, and then the grilled fish for two. One more thing: my husband is allergic to nuts. Is there anything with nuts in the fish?
Thank you for telling me. Let me ask the kitchen... No nuts. It is safe.
And later, would you like a dessert? We have the chocolate cake and the orange tart.
Yes, please. I would like the orange tart. It is lighter than the cake, and the main course is big.
And could we have the bill, please? I used to wait for my husband to ask, but tonight I am asking.

Good evening, and welcome to A Cozinha do Porto. My name is Nuno, and I am your waiter tonight. Here is the menu. Before you order, let me tell you about our specials. For the appetizer, I recommend the tomato soup. It is very light, and it is smaller than the cheese plate, so you still have room for the main course. For the main course, the grilled fish is our special tonight. It is fresh, it comes from the sea this morning, and it is cheaper than the beef. The beef is bigger, and it is our most popular dish, but it is heavier. Would you like still water or sparkling water with your meal? And for dessert, we have the chocolate cake and the orange tart. The tart is lighter than the cake, and after a big main course, many people would like something light. One more thing, and this is important: if you are allergic to something, please tell me now, before you order. We can change the dish for you, and the kitchen is very careful. I am coming back in five minutes. Take your time with the menu.

Hello. My name is Marta, and I am at the next table, with my sister. Is this your first time here? The food is very good. Can I tell you something? Twenty years ago, I never opened a menu in a restaurant. I used to wait for my husband, because he always ordered for me. I used to say, the same, please, and that was all. It is true! But then I started traveling, and everything changed. Now I read the menu, I ask the waiter what he recommends, and I order what I want. I used to be afraid of the bill, too. In Portugal the bill is different, and the tip is smaller than in the United States. Here you leave five or ten percent, and it is enough. Nobody is angry. And the food? I used to eat only meat. I never ate fish, never. Now I love it: the fish here is fresher than at home, and it is cheaper than the meat. My sister says I am a different person now. She is right. Look, your waiter is coming. Enjoy your dinner!
